title: T-018 — UI: RPT-004 QC Holds Dashboard (/reporting/qc-holds) — shift/line filter + AM/PM split + critical alert (12-REPORTING §15.1 RPT-004, §10.1)
prompt:
# T-018 — RPT-004 QC Holds Dashboard

PRD: docs/prd/12-REPORTING-PRD.md §15.1 RPT-004, §10.1 (QC Holds dashboard display spec), §4.1 #4
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira
Prototype: prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/reporting/kpi-screens.jsx:5-166 (`rpt_qc_holds`)
UX: prototypes/design/12-REPORTING-UX.md:343-386

## Goal
Server Component page `/reporting/qc-holds` showing: holds table (Line / Product / Reason / Boxes Held / Boxes Rejected / Staff / Labour Hours / AM-PM); summary card (total boxes held, total labour, critical count); AM/PM shift summary cards with horizontal bar charts; critical-holds alert banner when count > 0; cross-module 'View in QA' drill-link (T-012 buildQualityHoldUrl). Empty state success message when no holds.

## Prototype parity
- Source: `kpi-screens.jsx:5-166` (`rpt_qc_holds`)
- Translation: RPT_QC_HOLDS mock → T-012 quality-holds consumer service helper; ChartBarH (AM/PM split) → Recharts BarChart horizontal layout; FreshnessStrip cadence='5 min' (mv_qc_holds_summary refresh cron per T-005); FilterChips + onSavePreset → opens save_preset_modal (T-026).

## Implementation contract
1. `apps/web/app/(reporting)/qc-holds/page.tsx` (RSC) — reads via T-012 getQcHoldsSummary; passes data to client.
2. Client component: filters (shift / line) via nuqs URL state; renders table + summary + AM/PM cards + critical alert.
3. Empty state: 'No QC holds today' success message with green checkmark per §10.1.
4. Critical holds alert banner: shown when critical.length > 0; data is server-computed via T-012 (`severity='critical'` rows) — never client-filtered.
5. Cross-module 'View in QA' link → buildQualityHoldUrl(holdId) per T-012.
6. Empty / loading / error / permission states; RTL + Playwright parity.
7. All labels via next-intl `reporting.qc_holds.*`.

## Files
- apps/web/app/(reporting)/qc-holds/page.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(reporting)/qc-holds/_components/qc-holds-client.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(reporting)/qc-holds/_components/am-pm-shift-cards.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(reporting)/qc-holds/__tests__/qc-holds.test.tsx [create]
- apps/web/messages/{en,pl}/reporting-qc-holds.json [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given mv_qc_holds_summary seeded with 5 holds (1 critical) for org_A, when /reporting/qc-holds renders, then table shows 5 rows, summary card shows critical=1, critical-alert banner renders.
2. Given URL ?shift=AM, when page renders, then table filters to AM-only rows and AM card highlighted; URL state survives reload.
3. Given critical.length=0, when page renders, then alert banner is absent (no DOM node) — not just hidden.
4. Given user clicks 'View in QA' on a hold row, when navigation occurs, then router.push('/quality/holds/<uuid>') per T-012 buildQualityHoldUrl.
5. Given no holds for the day (empty MV result), when page renders, then 'No QC holds today' success message with green checkmark per §10.1.
6. Given T-012 reports `available: false` (MV not yet refreshed), when page renders, then 'Data refreshing' loading state shown (graceful degradation).
7. Given user without rpt.dashboard.view, when accessing /reporting/qc-holds, then middleware T-009 403s + audits.
8. UI parity + 4 states + next-intl wrapping.

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: RTL asserts parity + filter behaviour + critical-alert conditional rendering + empty-state success message + degradation path + cross-module link.
- Playwright parity per prototype.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web test qc-holds && pnpm --filter @monopilot/web playwright test reporting-qc-holds`.
- GREEN: build page + client + AM/PM cards.

## Risk red lines
- Do not filter critical holds client-side — server-side via T-012 (translation-notes red line).
- Do not let 'View in QA' link accept raw holdId — UUID validation via buildQualityHoldUrl.
- Do not skip success empty-state per §10.1.
- Do not paste prototype JSX verbatim.
- Do not skip next-intl wrapping.
- Do not skip 4 states.
- Do not write to 09-QA tables from this page — consumer pattern only.
